title: Hunt, Our English Coasts
description: Test your knowledge of this object.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did Hunt change the title of this painting for its exhibition in France at the Universal Exposition of 1855?**  

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/814c004ea179c986c27f32a0f117a346.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Hunt's subject matter of the unguarded innocent sheep can be variously interpreted in nationalistic or religious terms.
**Hunt changed the painting’s title to *Strayed Sheep* when it was exhibited in the 1855 Universal Exposition in Paris, presumably to neutralize its political content and nationalistic overtones.  **

choices.content: To appeal to French Catholic audiences
To highlight his interest in nature

assessment_items.question_content:
**What contemporary political event may have motivated Hunt's concerns over England's threat of invasion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Hunt’s painting reflects his concerns over the safety of England in light of Napoleon III’s recent rise to power in France.**

choices.content: Napoleon III's rise to power in France
Napoleon's defeat by the Duke of Wellington
Conflicts in the Church of England

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does *Our English Coasts ("Strayed Sheep")* reflect formal interests of the Pre-Raphaelite artists?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/814c004ea179c986c27f32a0f117a346.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In contrast to a painting's content or interpretation, formal interests involve the visual elements in a work of art.
Hunt's painting reflects the formal interests of the Pre-Raphaelites to faithfully render natural details and to paint on a white ground to achieve bright vivid colors.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which explains why Hunt worked so long on this painting?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/814c004ea179c986c27f32a0f117a346.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: It was around the time of this painting that tube paints were first produced.  This innovation made it possible for artist to take their supplies outside and paint *en plein air*.

Hunt's interest in capturing the effects of natural light led to him to paint *Our English Coasts ("Strayed Sheep")* outdoors, but summer storms and bad weather slowed work on the painting which was finally completed in November.

choices.content: Storms and bad weather prevented him from painting outside.
Political tensions made it difficult to paint at the site.
Paint in tubes was not yet available for plein air painting.
His desire for accuracy in details caused him to excessively rework the canvas.

assessment_items.question_content:
**A religious interpretation of Hunt's painting would point to  [[☃ dropdown 1]] to support its validity. ** 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/814c004ea179c986c27f32a0f117a346.jpg)
hints: The painting may refer to internal conflicts at the time in the Church of England, which Hunt suggests has left the English people without spiritual or moral guidance.

**Jesus Christ used the metaphor of a shepherd watching over his flock to describe his relationship to Christian followers.  A religious interpretation of *Our English Coasts ("Strayed Sheep")* would rely on this traditional symbolism and point to the untended sheep in Hunt's painting, left to wander or fall stray without the protection of a shepherd.   **
